notes: Direct deps: root -> my-utils (local/file:, editable=false) and root -> my-helpers (local/link:, editable=true).
Transitive deps: my-utils -> tslib@2.8.1 (registry). tslib is the only registry-sourced package.
my-helpers has no dependencies — its sub-tree is empty.
Mend may flatten all local deps to editable=true or editable=false. Either direction is a failure: the editable flag is the ONLY observable difference between file: and link: in the dep tree.

local_source_edges.name: my-utils
local_source_edges.install_behavior: copy — Bun copies the package directory into node_modules at install time. Changes to vendor/my-utils do NOT reflect automatically.

local_source_edges.name: my-helpers
local_source_edges.install_behavior: symlink — Bun creates a symlink in node_modules pointing to vendor/my-helpers. Changes to vendor/my-helpers reflect immediately without reinstall.

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. Bun has no entry in Mend's install-tool list and does not appear in the UA javascript.md resolver file. Detection relies entirely on static bun.lock parsing (text JSONC, Bun 1.2+ format). The UA will likely fall back to the npm resolver; that resolver does not understand file:/link: protocols in bun.lock format.

CRITICAL — editable flag distinguishes file: from link:. Both my-utils (file:) and my-helpers (link:) map to source='local'. The ONLY correct differentiator is editable=false for file: and editable=true for link:. If Mend emits editable=true for both, or editable=false for both, or omits editable for both, the bundled-pattern failure mode is triggered.

tslib@2.8.1 is a transitive dep reachable only through my-utils (the file: local package). If tslib is missing from Mend output, that is a false negative — Mend failed to follow the dep chain through a local file: package into its registry transitive.
my-utils appears in the bun.lock workspaces section (not the packages section). A Mend parser that only reads the packages object will silently drop my-utils and its tslib transitive.
my-helpers appears in the bun.lock workspaces section (not the packages section). Same risk as my-utils: a packages-only parser will drop it.

Bun's file: protocol is semantically closer to npm's 'file:' (which creates a copy at install time). The link: protocol is Bun-specific (yarn compat) and creates a symlink. npm-resolver fallback does not understand link: — if the UA falls back to npm ls, link: will either be dropped or reported as a registry dep with a missing resolution URL.
